description: Here's a twist on your usual Bloody Mary. This is enough for four drinks to share with your brunch buddies. Prepare them the night before serving for optimum flavor.
ingredients: 4 dashes hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®), or to taste
directions: Pour dill pickle juice into a shallow plate. Combine sriracha salt, celery seed, and black pepper on another shallow plate.

One-by-one, dip the rims of 4 glasses first into the dill pickle juice and then into the salt mixture to coat the rims. Place glasses into the refrigerator or freezer. Pour remaining dill pickle juice and salt mixture into a large pitcher. Add vegetable juice, vodka, lime juice, horseradish powder, and hot sauce. Mix well. Allow Bloody Mary mixture to rest for at least 1 hour in the refrigerator.
Slide 1 pickled egg, 1 pickled okra spear, and 1 dill pickle onto each bamboo skewer.

Before serving, add 2 two ice cubes, 1 loaded bamboo skewer, and 1 celery stalk to each rimmed glass. Give the Bloody Mary pitcher a stir and pour evenly into the 4 prepared glasses. Serve immediately.
ingredient_text: dill pickle juice sriracha salt celery seed ½ ground black pepper vegetable juice such as v ¾ vodka freshly squeezed lime juice ¼ powdered horseradish dashes hot pepper sauce such as tabasco taste bamboo skewers pickled eggs pickled okra spears dill pickles ice cubes celery stalks
directions_text: pour dill pickle juice into a shallow plate. combine sriracha salt, celery seed, and black pepper on another shallow plate. one-by-one, dip the rims of 4 glasses first into the dill pickle juice and then into the salt mixture to coat the rims. place glasses into the refrigerator or freezer. pour remaining dill pickle juice and salt mixture into a large pitcher. add vegetable juice, vodka, lime juice, horseradish powder, and hot sauce. mix well. allow bloody mary mixture to rest for at least 1 hour in the refrigerator. slide 1 pickled egg, 1 pickled okra spear, and 1 dill pickle onto each bamboo skewer. before serving, add 2 two ice cubes, 1 loaded bamboo skewer, and 1 celery stalk to each rimmed glass. give the bloody mary pitcher a stir and pour evenly into the 4 prepared glasses. serve immediately.

Pour remaining dill pickle juice and salt mixture into a large pitcher. Add vegetable juice, vodka, lime juice, horseradish powder, and hot sauce. Mix well. description: This is my Wisconsin version of a classic Bloody Mary drink. On special occasions, garnish with skewered bite-sized pieces of Wisconsin Cheddar, Monterey Jack, pepperoni, salami, green olives, or cocktail shrimp. Best when consumed before noon.
ingredients: ½ teaspoon celery salt, plus more for garnish
3 dashes hot pepper sauce (e.g. Texas Pete's)
1 cup tomato and clam juice cocktail
directions: Fill a large tumbler with ice cubes. Pour in vodka, then add olives and brine. Season with celery salt, pepper, Worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce. Top with tomato and clam juice cocktail. Stir with celery stalk and leave in as a garnish. Sprinkle with additional celery salt before serving.
ingredient_text: ice cubes fluid ounce jigger vodka pimento-stuffed green olives brine from olive jar ½ celery salt plus more for garnish ½ ground black pepper dashes worcestershire sauce dashes hot pepper sauce e texas pete s tomato clam juice cocktail stalk celery with leaves

Top with tomato and clam juice cocktail. Stir with celery stalk and leave in as a garnish. Sprinkle with additional celery salt before serving.

dashes hot pepper sauce e texas pete s

description: This Bloody Maria is a perfect example of the popular Bloody Mary with a twist, with tequila instead of vodka and Tajin seasoning instead of celery salt.
directions: Place Tajin on a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into Tajin. Set aside.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add tomato juice, tequila, lime juice, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, horseradish, and celery seed. Shake until the outside of shaker is frosted; strain into the prepared glass.
ingredient_text: tajin fluid ounces tomato juice fluid ounces tequila lime juice dashes worcestershire sauce dashes hot sauce prepared horseradish celery seed
directions_text: place tajin on a plate. moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. press the moistened rim into tajin. set aside. fill a cocktail shaker with ice. add tomato juice, tequila, lime juice, worcestershire sauce, tabasco, horseradish, and celery seed. shake until the outside of shaker is frosted; strain into the prepared glass.
combined_text: bloody maria bloody marys bloody marys 1/2 teaspoon tajin®) 4 fluid ounces tomato juice 2 fluid ounces tequila 1 teaspoon lime juice 2 dashes worcestershire sauce 2 dashes hot sauce 1/2 teaspoon prepared horseradish 1/4 teaspoon celery seed place tajin on a plate. moisten the rim of a glass with a lime wedge. press the moistened rim into tajin. set aside. fill a cocktail shaker with ice. add tomato juice, tequila, lime juice, worcestershire sauce, tabasco, horseradish, and celery seed. shake until the outside of shaker is frosted; strain into the prepared glass.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice. Add tomato juice, tequila, lime juice, Worcestershire sauce, Tabasco, horseradish, and celery seed. Shake until the outside of shaker is frosted; strain into the prepared glass.

description: The Bloody Caesar, an iconic Canadian cocktail simply referred to as a Caesar in Canada, is a national favorite, known for its distinctive flavor that sets it apart from the classic Bloody Mary. It combines vodka with Clamato juice (a savory mix of clam and tomato juice) for a distinct briny, unami taste. Spiced with hot sauce and Worcestershire sauce, it's usually garnished with a celery stick, lime wedge, pickled treats such as beans or olives, or whatever your heart desires.
directions: Sprinkle celery salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill glass with ice.

Add vodka, Worcestershire sauce, and Tabasco sauce, then fill with Clamato juice and gently stir. Sprinkle in a pinch of celery salt, then garnish with lime wedge, celery stick and other preferred garnishes.
ingredient_text: lime wedge celery salt ice fluid ounces vodka dashes worcestershire sauce dashes tabasco fluid ounces clamato celery stick plus other garnishes
directions_text: sprinkle celery salt onto a plate. moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. press the moistened rim into the salt. fill glass with ice. add vodka, worcestershire sauce, and tabasco sauce, then fill with clamato juice and gently stir. sprinkle in a pinch of celery salt, then garnish with lime wedge, celery stick and other preferred garnishes.
combined_text: bloody caesar bloody marys bloody marys 1 lime wedge 1 tablespoon celery salt, 1 cup ice 2 fluid ounces vodka 4 dashes worcestershire sauce 2 dashes tabasco 8 fluid ounces clamato 1 celery stick, plus other garnishes sprinkle celery salt onto a plate. moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. press the moistened rim into the salt. fill glass with ice. add vodka, worcestershire sauce, and tabasco sauce, then fill with clamato juice and gently stir. sprinkle in a pinch of celery salt, then garnish with lime wedge, celery stick and other preferred garnishes.
directions_raw: Sprinkle celery salt onto a plate. Moisten the rim of a glass with lime wedge. Press the moistened rim into the salt. Fill glass with ice.

Add vodka, Worcestershire sauce, and Tabasco sauce, then fill with Clamato juice and gently stir. Sprinkle in a pinch of celery salt, then garnish with lime wedge, celery stick and other preferred garnishes.

description: A Bloody Mary is easy to make from scratch with vodka, tomato juice, and a few other simple ingredients. The spicy, salty, and savory taste of this classic cocktail makes it perfect for brunch or other afternoon gatherings.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon sea salt, or as needed
¾ cup spicy tomato-vegetable juice cocktail (such as V8®)
1 dash hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
salt and ground black pepper to taste
2 garlic-stuffed green olives, threaded onto a toothpick
directions: Gather all ingredients.
Pour salt onto a small plate. Moisten the rim of a glass and press into the salt. Fill the glass with ice cubes.

Fill a cocktail shaker with ice cubes; add vegetable juice cocktail, vodka, Worcestershire sauce, hot pepper sauce, salt, and pepper. Cover and shake until the outside of shaker has frosted, about 20 seconds.
Strain Bloody Mary into the prepared glass. Garnish with celery stalk and olives.
Enjoy!

description: This classic Caesar drink is very popular in Canada. Take a lick of the celery salt, then a sip of the Caesar through the straw for the proper Caesar experience.
ingredients: 1 tablespoon celery salt, or as needed
1 dash Worcestershire sauce, or to taste
1 dash hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®), or to taste
8 fluid ounces tomato and clam juice cocktail (such as Clamato®)
directions: Gather ingredients.
Wet the rim of a cocktail glass with lime wedge; set lime wedge aside for garnish.
Place celery salt in a small dish; press the rim of the glass into salt to coat. Add ice to the glass.
Pour vodka, Worcestershire sauce, and hot pepper sauce over ice.
Top with tomato-clam juice.
Garnish with lime wedge and celery stick. Serve with a straw.

description: Spice up your brunch with this lively, eye-opening cocktail, which delivers a bold, flavorful punch that'll awaken your senses and kick-start your day.
ingredients: 1 ½ cups ice cubes, more as needed
¼ fluid ounce fresh lemon juice
4 dashes hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
1 pinch salt and ground black pepper
directions: Fill a pint glass or goblet with 1 1/2 cups ice. Set aside.

Combine tomato juice, vodka, lemon juice, hot pepper sauce, Worcestershire sauce, salt, black pepper, and 1 cup ice in a mixing glass. Stir until chilled and strain into the reserved ice-filled pint glass or goblet.
Garnish with celery stalk.

recipe_title: Spicy Red Snapper (Bloody Mary with Gin)
description: I've tried many variations, and decided that these ingredients make the very best Red Snapper out there. It is now my go-to drink, and friends absolutely love it - even those who don't like gin. Careful! Your lips might burn, but it sure goes down easy...
ingredients: ½ teaspoon brine from olive jar
directions: Place ice cubes into a cocktail shaker, and pour in tomato juice, gin, lemon juice, hot sauce, and Worcestershire sauce. Season with chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, celery salt, pepper, and horseradish. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Pour contents (including ice) into a 12 ounce highball glass. Garnish with celery, pickle, olives, and lemon wedge before serving.
ingredient_text: ice cubes fluid ounce can tomato juice fluid ounces dry gin fresh lemon juice hot pepper sauce worcestershire sauce ½ brine from olive jar pinch hot chili powder pinch garlic powder pinch ground cumin ¼ celery salt ¼ fresh-ground black pepper ¼ prepared horseradish celery stalk small dill pickle stuffed green olives wedge lemon

Season with chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, celery salt, pepper, and horseradish. Cover and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Pour contents (including ice) into a 12 ounce highball glass. Garnish with celery, pickle, olives, and lemon wedge before serving.

description: My father-in-law first introduced me to this homemade bloody mary mix. It is very similar to what you'd buy in a store. Made as is, it is very spicy. You can customize it to your taste by cutting back on the pepper and hot sauce; I like about half the pepper and hot sauce. Enjoy this wonderful mix! It is so easy and much cheaper than buying pre-made mix.
ingredients: ½ (10.5 ounce) can condensed beef consomme (such as Campbell's ®)
1 tablespoon hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
directions: Mix the tomato juice, beef consomme, Worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, hot pepper sauce, celery salt, and black pepper together in a pitcher; chill.

directions_text: mix the tomato juice, beef consomme, worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, hot pepper sauce, celery salt, and black pepper together in a pitcher; chill.

description: Mother-in-law gave me this recipe. It tastes better then the name brand store stuff. Spice it up or down to your tastes. Chill and mix with desired amount of vodka and ice.
directions: Stir tomato juice, lime juice, pickled jalapeno pepper juice, vinegar, white sugar, Worcestershire sauce, horseradish, salt, pepper, cloves, onion powder, and garlic powder in a 2-quart pitcher.

directions: Rim a glass with lime, kosher salt and chili powder.
Fill the glass with ice and add ingredients. Mix the ingredients thoroughly, then garnish with a fresh celery stick and lime wedge and enjoy!

recipe_title: Ripley's Bloody Mary Mix for Canning
description: Bloody Mary mornings are a neighborhood tradition. Now we can get a morning started right with this Ripley's Bloody Mary mix for canning mix straight from the garden!
ingredients: 1 teaspoon seafood seasoning (such as Old Bay)
1 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco)
8 (1 quart) sterilized canning jars with lids and rings
directions: Grind chile de arbol peppers into a powder with a mortar and pestle.
Process tomatoes, onions, green bell peppers, carrots, garlic, and jalapeño pepper in a food processor, working in batches, until vegetables are finely chopped; transfer to a large pot.

Stir tomato sauce, vinegar, horseradish, bay leaves, ground chile de arbol peppers, kosher salt, Worcestershire sauce, black pepper, celery seed, seafood seasoning, and hot pepper sauce into vegetables; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer, stirring regularly, until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes; remove bay leaves.

Process mixture through a vegetable juicer, working in batches if necessary; strain excess pulp from juice if desired. Pour into a large pot over medium heat, and cook until juice is nearly boiling; stir in lemon juice.

Sterilize jars and lids in boiling water for at least 5 minutes. Pack Bloody Mary mix into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within 1/4 inch of the top. Run a knife or a thin spatula around the insides of the jars after they have been filled to remove any air bubbles. Wipe the rims of the jars with a moist paper towel to remove any food residue. Top with lids, and screw on rings.

Place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot or canning kettle and fill halfway with water; bring to a boil. Lower jars into the boiling water using a holder, leaving a 2-inch space between jars. Pour in more boiling water if necessary to bring the water level to at least 1 inch above the tops of the jars. Bring the water to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 40 minutes (or 35 minutes if using pint-sized jars).

Remove jars from the pot and place onto a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart. Cool. Press the top of each lid with a finger, ensuring that the seal is tight. Store in a cool, dark area.
ingredient_text: dried chile de arbol peppers pounds tomatoes chopped pounds onions chopped green bell peppers chopped carrots chopped cloves garlic minced jalapeño pepper chopped ounce cans tomato sauce vinegar prepared horseradish bay leaves kosher salt worcestershire sauce freshly ground black pepper celery seed seafood seasoning such as old bay hot pepper sauce such as tabasco lemon juice quart sterilized canning jars with lids rings
directions_text: grind chile de arbol peppers into a powder with a mortar and pestle. process tomatoes, onions, green bell peppers, carrots, garlic, and jalapeño pepper in a food processor, working in batches, until vegetables are finely chopped; transfer to a large pot. stir tomato sauce, vinegar, horseradish, bay leaves, ground chile de arbol peppers, kosher salt, worcestershire sauce, black pepper, celery seed, seafood seasoning, and hot pepper sauce into vegetables; bring to a boil. reduce heat, and simmer, stirring regularly, until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes; remove bay leaves. process mixture through a vegetable juicer, working in batches if necessary; strain excess pulp from juice if desired. pour into a large pot over medium heat, and cook until juice is nearly boiling; stir in lemon juice. sterilize jars and lids in boiling water for at least 5 minutes. pack bloody mary mix into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within 1/4 inch of the top. run a knife or a thin spatula around the insides of the jars after they have been filled to remove any air bubbles. wipe the rims of the jars with a moist paper towel to remove any food residue. top with lids, and screw on rings. place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot or canning kettle and fill halfway with water; bring to a boil. lower jars into the boiling water using a holder, leaving a 2-inch space between jars. pour in more boiling water if necessary to bring the water level to at least 1 inch above the tops of the jars. bring the water to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 40 minutes (or 35 minutes if using pint-sized jars). remove jars from the pot and place onto a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart. cool. press the top of each lid with a finger, ensuring that the seal is tight. store in a cool, dark area.
combined_text: ripley's bloody mary mix for canning bloody marys bloody marys 4 dried chile de arbol peppers 20 pounds tomatoes, chopped 2 pounds onions, chopped 4 green bell peppers, chopped 4 carrots, chopped 4 cloves garlic, minced 1 jalapeño pepper, chopped 2 (8 ounce) cans tomato sauce 1 cup vinegar 6 tablespoons prepared horseradish 5 bay leaves 4 teaspoons kosher salt 4 teaspoons worcestershire sauce 2 teaspoons freshly ground black pepper 1 teaspoon celery seed 1 teaspoon seafood seasoning (such as old bay) 1 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (such as tabasco) 1 cup lemon juice 8 (1 quart) sterilized canning jars with lids and rings grind chile de arbol peppers into a powder with a mortar and pestle. process tomatoes, onions, green bell peppers, carrots, garlic, and jalapeño pepper in a food processor, working in batches, until vegetables are finely chopped; transfer to a large pot. stir tomato sauce, vinegar, horseradish, bay leaves, ground chile de arbol peppers, kosher salt, worcestershire sauce, black pepper, celery seed, seafood seasoning, and hot pepper sauce into vegetables; bring to a boil. reduce heat, and simmer, stirring regularly, until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes; remove bay leaves. process mixture through a vegetable juicer, working in batches if necessary; strain excess pulp from juice if desired. pour into a large pot over medium heat, and cook until juice is nearly boiling; stir in lemon juice. sterilize jars and lids in boiling water for at least 5 minutes. pack bloody mary mix into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within 1/4 inch of the top. run a knife or a thin spatula around the insides of the jars after they have been filled to remove any air bubbles. wipe the rims of the jars with a moist paper towel to remove any food residue. top with lids, and screw on rings. place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot or canning kettle and fill halfway with water; bring to a boil. lower jars into the boiling water using a holder, leaving a 2-inch space between jars. pour in more boiling water if necessary to bring the water level to at least 1 inch above the tops of the jars. bring the water to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 40 minutes (or 35 minutes if using pint-sized jars). remove jars from the pot and place onto a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart. cool. press the top of each lid with a finger, ensuring that the seal is tight. store in a cool, dark area.
ingredients_raw: 1 teaspoon seafood seasoning (such as Old Bay)
1 teaspoon hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco)
8 (1 quart) sterilized canning jars with lids and rings
directions_raw: Grind chile de arbol peppers into a powder with a mortar and pestle.
Process tomatoes, onions, green bell peppers, carrots, garlic, and jalapeño pepper in a food processor, working in batches, until vegetables are finely chopped; transfer to a large pot.

Stir tomato sauce, vinegar, horseradish, bay leaves, ground chile de arbol peppers, kosher salt, Worcestershire sauce, black pepper, celery seed, seafood seasoning, and hot pepper sauce into vegetables; bring to a boil. Reduce heat, and simmer, stirring regularly, until vegetables are tender, about 30 minutes; remove bay leaves.

Process mixture through a vegetable juicer, working in batches if necessary; strain excess pulp from juice if desired. Pour into a large pot over medium heat, and cook until juice is nearly boiling; stir in lemon juice.

Sterilize jars and lids in boiling water for at least 5 minutes. Pack Bloody Mary mix into hot, sterilized jars, filling to within 1/4 inch of the top. Run a knife or a thin spatula around the insides of the jars after they have been filled to remove any air bubbles. Wipe the rims of the jars with a moist paper towel to remove any food residue. Top with lids, and screw on rings.

Place a rack in the bottom of a large stockpot or canning kettle and fill halfway with water; bring to a boil. Lower jars into the boiling water using a holder, leaving a 2-inch space between jars. Pour in more boiling water if necessary to bring the water level to at least 1 inch above the tops of the jars. Bring the water to a rolling boil, cover, and process for 40 minutes (or 35 minutes if using pint-sized jars).

Remove jars from the pot and place onto a cloth-covered or wood surface, several inches apart. Cool. Press the top of each lid with a finger, ensuring that the seal is tight. Store in a cool, dark area.
ingredients_canonical: seafood seasoning such as old bay
hot pepper sauce such as tabasco
quart sterilized canning jars with lids rings

description: This recipe is restaurant quality. You will not be disappointed!
ingredients: ¼ teaspoon hot sauce such as Tabasco
1 tablespoon seafood seasoning, such as Old Bay™
directions: Pour the tomato juice, hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, dill, seafood seasoning, celery salt, and vodka into a cocktail shaker over ice. Cover, and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into ice-filled glasses, and garnish with cornichons to serve.

ingredients_raw: ¼ teaspoon hot sauce such as Tabasco
1 tablespoon seafood seasoning, such as Old Bay™
directions_raw: Pour the tomato juice, hot sauce, Worcestershire sauce, dill, seafood seasoning, celery salt, and vodka into a cocktail shaker over ice. Cover, and shake until the outside of the shaker has frosted. Strain into ice-filled glasses, and garnish with cornichons to serve.

recipe_title: Dani's Butt Kickin' Bloody Mary Mix
description: When a crazy old lady hits you in the head with her purse...and she's carrying a Colt 45 in it...that's the kind of kick you want when you drink a bloody mary. I like it spicy, and I hope you do too. Add vodka and whatever garnishes you prefer. Allow to chill for at least 8 hours so that the evil inside can mature. Drink it down, enjoy the heat, and be nice to your grandma.
ingredients: 1 (46 fluid ounce) bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail
2 tablespoons hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
directions: Mix vegetable juice cocktail, clam juice, Worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce, lemon juice, brown sugar, beef base, celery salt, and black pepper in a large pitcher, stirring until brown sugar and celery salt have dissolved. Allow to chill for at least 8 hours.
ingredient_text: fluid ounce bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail ounce bottle clam juice low-salt worcestershire sauce prepared horseradish hot pepper sauce such as tabasco lemon juice brown sugar concentrated beef base paste celery salt ground black pepper
directions_text: mix vegetable juice cocktail, clam juice, worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce, lemon juice, brown sugar, beef base, celery salt, and black pepper in a large pitcher, stirring until brown sugar and celery salt have dissolved. allow to chill for at least 8 hours.
combined_text: dani's butt kickin' bloody mary mix bloody marys bloody marys 1 (46 fluid ounce) bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail 1 (8 ounce) bottle clam juice 6 tablespoons low-salt worcestershire sauce 6 tablespoons prepared horseradish 2 tablespoons hot pepper sauce (such as tabasco®) 2 tablespoons lemon juice 1 tablespoon brown sugar 1 tablespoon concentrated beef base (paste) 1 tablespoon celery salt 1 teaspoon ground black pepper mix vegetable juice cocktail, clam juice, worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce, lemon juice, brown sugar, beef base, celery salt, and black pepper in a large pitcher, stirring until brown sugar and celery salt have dissolved. allow to chill for at least 8 hours.
ingredients_raw: 1 (46 fluid ounce) bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail
2 tablespoons hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®)
directions_raw: Mix vegetable juice cocktail, clam juice, Worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce, lemon juice, brown sugar, beef base, celery salt, and black pepper in a large pitcher, stirring until brown sugar and celery salt have dissolved. Allow to chill for at least 8 hours.

hot pepper sauce such as tabasco

description: Calling all pickle lovers! Kick up your Bloody Mary with loads of pickle flavor! Serve with your favorite garnishes - the possibilities are endless! I like to serve with a skewer of pickled onion, pickled jalapeno, olive, and a mini dill. The flavor of pickle brine varies depending on brand, so start with 1 ounce, and if a more pronounced pickle flavor is desired, add another ounce.
ingredients: 1 teaspoon chili-lime seasoning, such as Tajin®), or to taste
2 cups ice cubes, or as needed, divided
4 ounces bottled Bloody Mary mix (such as Zing Zang®)
2 dashes hot pepper sauce (such as Tabasco®), or to taste
directions: Pour chili-lime seasoning onto a small plate. Rub the edge of a pint-sized jar with 1 lime wedge. Dip the edge of the jar into the seasoning. Fill jar with 1 cup ice and set aside.
Fill a cocktail shaker with remaining cup of ice, Bloody Mary mix, pickle juice, vodka, Worcestershire, and hot sauce. Squeeze in 1 lime wedge and 1 lemon wedge.

Roughly chop 1 fresh dill sprig and add to the shaker. Place lid on the shaker and shake for 10 seconds to combine. Strain into the prepared jar and garnish with pickle spear and remaining dill, lime, and lemon.

description: Add some zest to your Sunday with a spicy Bloody Mary recipe. Savory flavors come together with smooth Pinnacle Original Vodka to create the perfect crowd-pleaser for football parties, Sunday brunch, or just a relaxing weekend afternoon.
directions: Combine all ingredients except celery in an ice-filled shaker and shake until well chilled.
Strain into a chilled pint glass over fresh ice.
Garnish with celery and any other fun toppings on hand.

description: My sister got this recipe from a restaurant in Ohio and all our family loves it. This is very spicy and needs time to blend flavors.
ingredients: 1 (46 fluid ounce) bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail
directions: In a large pitcher, combine juice cocktail, lemon juice and brown sugar. Season with Worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce and celery salt. Cover, and refrigerate 8 to 12 hours to allow flavors to meld.
ingredient_text: fluid ounce bottle tomato-vegetable juice cocktail ½ medium lemons juiced brown sugar steak sauce worcestershire sauce prepared horseradish hot pepper sauce ½ celery salt
directions_text: in a large pitcher, combine juice cocktail, lemon juice and brown sugar. season with worcestershire sauce, horseradish, hot sauce and celery salt. cover, and refrigerate 8 to 12 hours to allow flavors to meld.
